# Key Patent Rules — 37 C.F.R. Part 1 (Operative Requirements Reference)

**Status:** US Government Works (public domain — free to reproduce).
**Current as of:** 2026-06-15.
**Primary source:** Legal Information Institute, Cornell Law School, mirroring the Code of Federal Regulations (`https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/37/<n>`). The eCFR canonical site (`https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-37`) was attempted first but redirected to a bot-wall (`unblock.federalregister.gov`); Cornell is the authoritative public-domain mirror used here and is one of the two sources the task authorized.

This file summarizes the **operative requirement** of each rule — the specific thing an APA (Authorized Patent Agent / examiner-side compliance check) would verify. Long rules are summarized faithfully and linked out; short operative phrases are quoted. Dollar amounts are intentionally omitted (they change with each fee-schedule rulemaking); fee *triggers* are described instead.

---

## § 1.16 — National application filing, search, and examination fees

- **Title:** Fees for filing, search, and examination.
- **Source:** https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/37/1.16
- **Operative requirement (what an APA checks — was the correct fee set paid for what was filed):**
  - **(a)–(e) Basic filing fee** — owed per application by type: (a) original utility, (b) design, (c) plant, (d) provisional, (e) reissue. Tiered by entity status (undiscounted / small / micro).
  - **(f) Late-payment surcharge** — triggered when the basic filing fee, search fee, examination fee, or inventor's oath/declaration is filed *after* the filing date, or when the application is filed without claims or by reference to a prior application.
  - **(g) Provisional late surcharge** — basic filing fee or cover sheet filed after the provisional's filing date.
  - **(h) Excess independent-claim fee** — owed "for each independent claim in excess of three."
  - **(i) Excess total-claim fee** — owed "for each claim (whether dependent or independent) in excess of twenty."
  - **(j) Multiple dependent claim fee** — owed once per application "containing a multiple dependent claim."
  - **(k)–(n) Search fees** — separate search fee per application type (utility / design / plant / reissue).
  - **(o)–(r) Examination fees** — separate examination fee per application type.
  - **(s) Application size fee** — quoted: owed "for the specification and drawings which exceed 100 sheets of paper, for each additional 50 sheets or fraction thereof." (Sheet count excludes any sequence listing / computer program listing filed in compliant electronic form.)
  - **(t) / (u) Non-electronic / non-DOCX fees** — a surcharge for utility applications **not** filed via the USPTO electronic filing system, and (effective Jan 17, 2024) a **non-DOCX filing fee** that applies when the specification, claims, and abstract of a utility nonprovisional are not filed in USPTO-compliant DOCX format.

---

## § 1.17 — Patent application processing fees

- **Title:** Patent application and reexamination processing fees.
- **Source:** https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/37/1.17
- **Operative requirement (correct processing fee paid for the act requested):**
  - **(a) Extension-of-time fees** — five graduated tiers for an extension under § 1.136(a) of a shortened statutory or nonstatutory reply period: first month (lowest) through fifth month (highest), each tiered by entity status. This is the fee that *enables* a § 1.136(a) extension.
  - **(c) Prioritized examination** — request under § 1.102(e) ("Track One").
  - **(e) Request for continued examination (RCE)** — first RCE under § 1.114; higher fee for second and subsequent RCEs in the same application.
  - **(f), (g), (h), (m) Petition fees** — graduated petition fees (e.g., revival of abandoned application, delayed priority/benefit claims, other petitions to the Director).
  - **(i) Processing fee** — administrative acts (correction of inventorship, conversion of application type, late/belated items).
  - **(p) IDS fee** — the fee referenced by § 1.97(c) and (d) that accompanies a late information disclosure statement.
  - **(v) IDS size fee** — cumulative fees that attach when the *number of items* cited across the application's IDSs exceeds 50, then 100, then 200.
  - **(u) Provisional extension fee** — extension-of-time fee tier for provisional applications.

---

## § 1.29 — Micro entity status

- **Title:** Micro entity status.
- **Source:** https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/37/1.29
- **Operative requirement (is the micro-entity certification supportable on one of two bases):**
  - **(a) Gross-income basis** — applicant must certify ALL of: (1) qualifies as a **small entity** under § 1.27; (2) "Neither the applicant nor the inventor nor a joint inventor has been named as the inventor or a joint inventor on more than four previously filed patent applications" (excluding provisionals, foreign applications, and assigned-away applications); (3) none of those persons had, in the calendar year preceding the fee, a gross income "exceeding three times the median household income" (the multiplier the USPTO Director publishes); and (4) no assignment/license/obligation to assign to an entity whose gross income exceeded that same threshold.
  - **(d) Institution-of-higher-education basis** — applicant certifies small-entity status AND either (i) the applicant's employer, from which the applicant obtains the majority of income, is an "institution of higher education," or (ii) the applicant has assigned/conveyed (or is obligated to assign) the invention to such an institution.
  - **Certification & loss of status** — "Micro entity status is established in an application by filing a micro entity certification in writing." A "notification of a loss of entitlement to micro entity status" must be filed prior to or with the next fee paid once the applicant no longer qualifies.

---

## § 1.46 — Application for patent by an applicant other than the inventor

- **Title:** Application for patent by an assignee, obligated assignee, or person who otherwise shows sufficient proprietary interest.
- **Source:** https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/37/1.46
- **Operative requirement (is the named applicant a permitted non-inventor applicant, properly identified and supported):**
  - The applicant may be the inventor(s) OR a non-inventor in three permitted categories: "(1) An assignee," "(2) A person to whom the inventor is under an obligation to assign the invention," or "(3) A person who otherwise shows sufficient proprietary interest in the matter."
  - The non-inventor applicant **must be identified in the application data sheet** under § 1.76 (applicant-information section).
  - **Documentary showing:** for an assignee/obligated assignee, documentary evidence of ownership (e.g., recorded assignment, employment agreement) must be **recorded no later than the date the issue fee is paid**; for a "sufficient proprietary interest" applicant, a **petition** including the fee, a showing of proprietary interest, and a statement that the action is "appropriate to preserve the rights of the parties."
  - A change to a different applicant requires a corrected ADS under § 1.76(c) and compliance with the § 3.71/3.73 ownership-establishment rules.

---

## § 1.52 — Language, paper, writing, margins, compact disc specifications (specification format)

- **Title:** Language, paper, writing, margins, read-only optical disc specifications.
- **Source:** https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/37/1.52
- **Operative requirement (does the paper/document meet physical and formatting standards — § 1.52(b)):**
  - **Paper:** "flexible, strong, smooth, non-shiny, durable, and white"; written on **only one side** in portrait orientation; in permanent dark ink.
  - **Sheet size:** "Either 21.0 cm by 29.7 cm (DIN size A4) or 21.6 cm by 27.9 cm (8 1/2 by 11 inches)." No holes in the sheets as submitted.
  - **Margins (specification):** "a top margin of at least 2.0 cm (3/4 inch), a left side margin of at least 2.5 cm (1 inch), a right side margin of at least 2.0 cm (3/4 inch), and a bottom margin of at least 2.0 cm (3/4 inch)."
  - **Line spacing:** "1 1/2 or double spaced."
  - **Font:** "nonscript type font (e.g., Arial, Times Roman, or Courier, preferably a font size of 12)" with capital letters "at least 0.3175 cm. (0.125 inch) high, but may be no smaller than 0.21 cm. (0.08 inch) high."
  - **Paragraph numbering (§ 1.52(b)(6)):** each paragraph "should be individually and consecutively numbered" — "The number should consist of at least four numerals enclosed in square brackets, including leading zeros (e.g., [0001])," appearing to the right of the left margin as the first item in the paragraph, highlighted in bold.

---

## § 1.56 — Duty to disclose information material to patentability

- **Title:** Duty to disclose information material to patentability (duty of candor and good faith).
- **Source:** https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/37/1.56
- **Operative requirement (was the duty of candor satisfied — material information disclosed):**
  - **Core duty (a):** "Each individual associated with the filing and prosecution of a patent application has a duty of candor and good faith in dealing with the Office, which includes a duty to disclose to the Office all information known to that individual to be material to patentability." No patent will be granted on an application "in connection with which fraud on the Office was practiced or attempted or the duty of disclosure was violated through bad faith or intentional misconduct."
  - **"Material to patentability" (b):** information not already of record that either (1) establishes, by itself or with other information, a **prima facie case of unpatentability** of a claim (preponderance standard, claims given their broadest reasonable construction), or (2) **refutes or is inconsistent with** a position the applicant takes in opposing a rejection or asserting patentability.
  - **Who owes the duty (c):** each named inventor; each attorney/agent who prepares or prosecutes the application; and every other person substantively involved in preparation/prosecution who is associated with the inventor, applicant, or assignee.

---

## § 1.63 — Inventor's oath or declaration

- **Title:** Inventor's oath or declaration.
- **Source:** https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/37/1.63
- **Operative requirement (does each inventor's oath/declaration contain the required statements):**
  - **Content (a):** must (1) "Identify the inventor or joint inventor executing the oath or declaration by his or her legal name"; (2) "Identify the application to which it is directed"; (3) include "a statement that the person executing the oath or declaration believes the named inventor or joint inventor to be the original inventor or an original joint inventor of a claimed invention in the application"; and (4) "State that the application was made or was authorized to be made by the person executing the oath or declaration."
  - **Inventor identification (b):** unless supplied in an ADS, the oath/declaration must identify each inventor by legal name and provide a mailing address and residence for each inventor.
  - **Review & disclosure duty (c):** the executing person must have reviewed and understood the contents of the application and be aware of the § 1.56 duty to disclose.
  - **False-statement warning (g):** acknowledgment that "any willful false statement made in such declaration or statement is punishable under 18 U.S.C. 1001 by fine or imprisonment of not more than five (5) years, or both."

---

## § 1.75 — Claim(s)

- **Title:** Claim(s).
- **Source:** https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/37/1.75
- **Operative requirement (do the claims meet form requirements):**
  - **(a):** "The specification must conclude with a claim particularly pointing out and distinctly claiming the subject matter which the applicant regards as the invention."
  - **(b):** "More than one claim may be presented provided they differ substantially from each other and are not unduly multiplied."
  - **(c) Dependent / multiple dependent claims:** a dependent claim incorporates by reference everything in the claim it depends on; "A multiple dependent claim shall not serve as a basis for any other multiple dependent claim," and a multiple dependent claim must "refer to such other claims in the alternative only."
  - **(e) Jepson (improvement) format:** where the invention is an improvement, the claim should contain (1) a preamble reciting what is conventional/known, (2) the transitional phrase "wherein the improvement comprises," and (3) the elements the applicant regards as the improvement.
  - **(i) Structure:** "Where a claim sets forth a plurality of elements or steps, each element or step of the claim should be separated by a line indentation." Each claim begins with a capital letter and ends with a period.

---

## § 1.76 — Application data sheet (ADS)

- **Title:** Application data sheet.
- **Source:** https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/37/1.76
- **Operative requirement (is the ADS present, complete, properly corrected, and controlling):**
  - **What it is / when required:** "a sheet or sheets" containing bibliographic data; an ADS is **required** when a domestic-benefit or foreign-priority claim is made under § 1.55 or § 1.78.
  - **Required sections (b):** (1) inventor information (legal name, residence, mailing address of each inventor); (2) correspondence information (address or customer number); (3) application information (title, total number of drawing sheets, application type); (4) representative information (practitioner registration number / customer number); (5) domestic benefit / national stage information; (6) foreign priority information (number, country/authority, filing date of each foreign application); (7) applicant information (name and address of the § 1.46 applicant).
  - **Corrections (c):** changes after the original filing are made by submitting "a new application data sheet" identifying the information being changed with markings — "underlining for insertions, and strike-through or brackets for text removed."
  - **Controls (d):** "The information in the application data sheet will govern when inconsistent with the information supplied at the same time" by another document (with limited exceptions for the inventor's oath and foreign-priority/benefit specifics).

---

## § 1.77 — Arrangement of application elements

- **Title:** Arrangement of application elements.
- **Source:** https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/37/1.77
- **Operative requirement (are the application parts and specification headings in the prescribed order):**
  - **(a) Element order** for a utility application: (1) utility application transmittal form; (2) fee transmittal form; (3) application data sheet (§ 1.76); (4) specification; (5) drawings; (6) inventor's oath or declaration.
  - **(b) Specification section order** (each preceded by an uppercase section heading, no underline/bold):
    1. Title of the invention
    2. Cross-reference to related applications
    3. Statement regarding federally sponsored research or development
    4. Names of the parties to a joint research agreement
    5. Reference to a sequence listing, a table, or a computer program listing compact disc appendix / incorporation-by-reference
    6. Statement regarding prior disclosures by the inventor or a joint inventor (grace-period disclosures)
    7. Background of the invention
    8. Brief summary of the invention
    9. Brief description of the several views of the drawing
    10. Detailed description of the invention
    11. Claim(s) — begins on a new page
    12. Abstract of the disclosure — on a separate sheet
    13. Sequence listing (where applicable)

---

## § 1.84 — Standards for drawings

- **Title:** Standards for drawings.
- **Source:** https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/37/1.84
- **Operative requirement (do the drawings meet USPTO drawing standards):**
  - **(a) Type of drawing — black-and-white line is the default:** "Black and white. Black ink. Black and white drawings are normally required." **Color drawings** are accepted only on a **petition** filed under § 1.84(a)(2) "explaining why the color drawings are necessary," together with the fee under § 1.17(h) and the required number of color sets/copies.
  - **(b) Photographs:** accepted only when they are "the only practicable medium for illustrating the claimed invention" (and B&W photos require a petition; color photos additionally require the § 1.84(a)(2) color petition).
  - **(c) Identification:** each sheet, within the top margin, should bear the title of the invention, inventor's name, and application number (or docket number if no application number yet).
  - **(g) Sheet margins (exact):** for A4 (21.0 cm × 29.7 cm) **and** 8.5 × 11 inch sheets — top "at least 2.5 cm. (1 inch)", left "at least 2.5 cm. (1 inch)", right "at least 1.5 cm. (5/8 inch)", bottom "at least 1.0 cm. (3/8 inch)." A usable surface no greater than 17.0 × 26.2 cm (A4) is required.
  - **(l) Character of lines:** "Every line, number, and letter must be durable, clean, black (except for color drawings), sufficiently dense and dark, and uniformly thick and well-defined."
  - **(p)(3) Reference characters / numbers / letters:** "must be at least .32 cm. (1/8 inch) in height" and must not be placed so as to interfere with comprehension.

---

## § 1.97 — Filing of information disclosure statement (IDS timing)

- **Title:** Filing of information disclosure statement.
- **Source:** https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/37/1.97
- **Operative requirement (was the IDS timely so the examiner must consider it — three timing tiers):**
  - **(b) No fee, no statement:** considered if filed within any of — within **three months of the filing date** (or national-stage entry date); before the mailing of a **first Office action on the merits**; or before the mailing of a first Office action after an RCE.
  - **(c) Statement OR fee:** if after the (b) window but **before** the mailing of a **final action, a notice of allowance, or an action that closes prosecution**, the IDS is considered only if accompanied by **either** the § 1.97(e) statement **or** the § 1.17(p) fee.
  - **(d) Statement AND fee:** if after the (c) window but **on or before payment of the issue fee**, the IDS is considered only if accompanied by **both** the § 1.97(e) statement **and** the § 1.17(p) fee.
  - **(e) The statement** certifies either: each item was first cited in a communication from a **foreign patent office** in a counterpart application **not more than three months** prior to the IDS filing; **or** no item was known to any individual having the § 1.56 duty more than three months prior.
  - **No extensions:** the IDS timing periods are **not extendable** under § 1.136.

---

## § 1.98 — Content of information disclosure statement (IDS content)

- **Title:** Content of information disclosure statement.
- **Source:** https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/37/1.98
- **Operative requirement (does the IDS contain the required list, copies, explanation, and fee assertion):**
  - **(a)(1) List:** "a list of all patents, publications, applications, or other information submitted for consideration by the Office," on a separate paper, identifying each item; **U.S. patents and U.S. patent application publications must be listed separately** from other documents. Each list page bears the application number, a column for the examiner's initials, and an IDS heading.
  - **(a)(2) Copies:** "a legible copy of" each foreign patent document, each non-patent publication (or relevant portion), each pending unpublished U.S. application relied on, and other information — **except** copies of U.S. patents and U.S. published applications need not be supplied (unless the Office requires them or they are not stored in IFW).
  - **(a)(3) Non-English items:** for any item not in the English language, a **concise explanation of the relevance** (as understood by the person submitting it) is required; and a copy of an English-language translation must be supplied if a written translation is within the possession, custody, or control of, or readily available to, any individual designated in § 1.56(c).
  - **Fee assertion:** the IDS must include a clear written assertion that it is accompanied by the applicable IDS size fee (§ 1.17(v)) or that no such fee is required.

---

## § 1.136 — Extensions of time

- **Title:** Extensions of time.
- **Source:** https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/37/1.136
- **Operative requirement (was a reply timely extended — automatic vs. for-cause):**
  - **(a) Automatic extensions:** obtained by filing a petition and paying the § 1.17(a)/(u) fee. An extension is available "up to the earlier of the expiration of any maximum period set by statute or five months after the time period set for reply." Practically: up to **5 additional months**, never beyond the **6-month statutory maximum** for reply (35 U.S.C. 133). The petition and fee may be filed **after** the reply but must be filed within the extended period (or the application is abandoned). A standing/constructive petition may be authorized in advance.
  - **Unavailable** where prohibited by statute, rule, or the action itself — e.g., reply-brief and oral-hearing periods, periods set by the Board, certain reexamination/derivation/contested-case situations. (A "FINAL" rejection is still extendable under (a) up to the 6-month cap.)
  - **(b) Extensions for cause:** when (a) is unavailable, an extension requires a petition showing **"sufficient cause"** for "a reasonable time specified," filed **on or before the day the reply is due**, with the § 1.17(g) fee; it is not automatic and cannot exceed the statutory maximum.
  - **(c) Non-extendable after allowance:** after a Notice of Allowability, the periods to submit the inventor's oath/declaration, corrected/formal drawings, and certain deposits are not extendable under (a).

---
